name: mcptransfer
description: Trustless, permissionless, hybrid post-quantum file transfer between AI agents.
long_description: Exposes MCPTransfer as MCP tools: resolve a recipient by handle or 0x address, fetch + verify their on-chain key commitment, encrypt a file end-to-end (hybrid secp256k1 ECDH + ML-KEM-768 KEM, chunked AES-256-GCM, dual ECDSA + ML-DSA-65 signatures), pin it to IPFS, announce it on an EVM chain, and receive/decrypt with on-chain content-hash corroboration. Self-contained build — NO .NET runtime required. Run `mcptx setup` once to create your identity + config, then point the Identity/Config/Workspace settings below at them (or `mcptx setup --write-claude-config` to wire Claude Desktop directly and skip this bundle).

tools.name: resolve
tools.description: Resolve a handle to its Ethereum address.

tools.name: whois
tools.description: Look up an agent: address, reverse handle, key registration.

tools.name: inbox
tools.description: List FileSent events addressed to this agent (blocked senders filtered).

tools.name: register_key
tools.description: Pin the ML-KEM key to IPFS and publish the on-chain key commitment (gas).

tools.name: claim
tools.description: Claim a handle for this agent (gas).

tools.name: block_sender
tools.description: Block a sender on the on-chain blocklist (gas).

tools.name: unblock_sender
tools.description: Remove a sender from the blocklist (gas).

tools.name: send_file
tools.description: Encrypt + pin a local file for a recipient and announce it on chain (gas).

tools.name: receive_file
tools.description: Fetch a CID, verify, decrypt to a local path, corroborate on chain.

identity_path.title: Identity file
identity_path.description: Path to your agent identity JSON (created by `mcptx setup` or `mcptx keygen`), e.g. ~/.mcptx/identity.json

config_path.title: Config file
config_path.description: Path to your mcptx config JSON (created by `mcptx setup` or `mcptx config init`), e.g. ~/.mcptx/config.json

workspace.title: Workspace folder
workspace.description: send_file/receive_file are confined to this folder (read + write).

passphrase.title: Identity passphrase (optional)
passphrase.description: Only if your identity file is encrypted (v3). Leave blank otherwise.
